candidate_mechanism.title: 3D Wiring And Dynamic Topology Division

equations.local_reuse_efficiency: E_local(r) = U_local(r) / (a_1 * r + a_2 * rho * r^3 + a_3 * crowding(r))
equations.bridge_efficiency: E_bridge = T_bridge / (b_1 * B_long + b_2 * delay_long + b_3 * interference_long)
equations.division_of_labor: E_total = eta_local * E_local + eta_bridge * E_bridge - eta_overlap * O_conflict
equations.dynamic_sparse_constraint: sum_{i,j} A_{ij} * d_{ij} <= W_budget, with A_{ij} activated only when demand-gap > prune-pressure

candidate_mechanism.core_claim: 3D efficiency comes from a division of labor: local 3D neighborhoods handle dense reusable feature formation, while a sparse dynamic long-range topology handles cross-region integration under a strict wiring budget.

interpretation.local_neighbor_count: in 3D, local candidate partners grow with volume, so local feature recombination becomes cheap and abundant
interpretation.local_reuse_efficiency: local coding wins when reuse rises faster than wire and crowding cost
interpretation.bridge_budget: long-range connections are scarce budgeted resources, not the default substrate
interpretation.bridge_efficiency: a bridge is only worthwhile if it carries high-value integration relative to delay and interference cost
interpretation.division_of_labor: the efficient brain does not use one topology for everything; it splits local reuse from sparse integration
interpretation.dynamic_sparse_constraint: plasticity decides when to spend long-range wiring budget, instead of geometry doing all the work by itself

project_readout.summary: P8B is positive only if the project can show that 3D efficiency is best explained by local reuse plus sparse dynamic bridges, rather than by one uniform geometry mechanism.
project_readout.next_question: If P8B holds, the next step is to derive sharp spatial brain-side falsifiers from this division of labor.
